Broncos star Reece Walsh is on track to line up in Saturday's showdown with the Roosters as a host of big names push to return to the field this weekend.

Raiders: Kaeo Weekes moves from fullback to five-eighth after Ethan Strange was ruled with an ankle injury. Daine Laurie is the new fullback. Owen Pattie has been added to the bench. In good news for the team, Simi Sasagi is also on track t

Titans: AJ Brimson returns in the centres after missing the Warriors game in Round 7 to remain in Australia for the birth of his second child. Brimson's return sees Jaylan de Groot move to the interchange. Chris Randall is back from a calf

Titans: Chris Randall is on track to return from a calf injury and will line up at lock if cleared to play. Randall's comeback will see Cooper Bai shift to the interchange. AJ Brimson is also back on deck after missing Round 7 for the birth

Sea Eagles: Veteran Nathan Brown is set for a recall after Corey Wadell suffered a pectoral injury that will keep him sidelined for up to 12 weeks. No other changes to a Manly side undefeated under interim coach Kieran Foran. Centre Reuben

Storm: Tyran Wishart takes over at halfback from Jahrome Hughes (concussion) while 20-year-old Hugo Peel has been named to make his NRL debut on the wing. Back-rowers Joe Chan, Cooper Clarke and Trent Loiero have been relegated to the bench

Knights: Big guns Kalyn Ponga (hamstring) and Bradman Best (groin) return in a huge double boost while winger Greg Marzhew (concussion) is also back on deck. Ponga's return sees Fletcher Sharpe move to five-eighth and Sandon Smith to the in

Panthers: Coach Ivan Cleary has the luxury of stability as the competition leaders return home to play the high-flying Sea Eagles. Cleary has named the same 19 that got the job done last week and it's unlikely he will make any late changes.
